title: Carbeth Plants White Hydrangea Macrophylla Plant 5L Pot - Hydrangea Plants Outdoor Garden Ready to Plant - 50-60cm Hydrangeas Potted Flowering Shrub
features: A Garden Favourite - The Hydrangea macrophylla, also known as Mophead Hydrangea, is among UK gardens' classic outdoor plant hydrangeas. It is loved for its long-lasting blooms and versatility. It is ideal for borders, patio pots, or as a statement shrub, bringing vibrant colour to any garden.
Thrives in UK Gardens – This hydrangea plant grows best in moist, well-drained soil with full sun to partial shade. Keep well-watered, but avoid waterlogged soil for healthy, vibrant flowers.
Important Growing Information – The bloom colour of blue and pink hydrangeas can change depending on the soil’s acidity. Acidic soil produces blue flowers, while alkaline soil encourages pink blooms.

Large, Garden-Ready Plant—Supplied in a 5-litre pot, this potted outdoor plant arrives strong and healthy. Please note that hydrangeas are cut back from November to April, so their seasonal appearance may vary.

Quality You Can Trust – At Carbeth Plants, we have over 20 years of expertise in providing high-quality garden plants. Our hydrangeas plants for the garden are carefully grown for strong roots and stunning, long-lasting blooms.

Seasonal Colour & Fragrance Offers vibrant blooms or aromatic foliage that enhance borders, patios and containers throughout the year. Wildlife Friendly Attracts bees, butterflies and birds, supporting pollinators and encouraging natural biodiversity. Low Maintenance & Hardy Easy to grow, drought tolerant and resilient in varied UK climates, ideal for beginners and busy gardeners. Long-Term Structure Theres a shrub for every garden. Bring year round interest with evergreen shrubbery, or a burst of colour in the growing season with seasonal flowering hedges. When is the best time to plant shrubs? The best time to plant shrubs is in autumn or early spring when the soil is moist but not waterlogged. This allows roots to establish before hot or frosty weather. Do shrubs need pruning? Light pruning once a year helps maintain shape and encourages new growth. Prune after flowering for spring-flowering shrubs and in late winter for summer bloomers. Can I grow shrubs in containers? Yes, many compact or slow growing shrubs thrive in pots. Use a quality compost and ensure good drainage to prevent root rot. How can I encourage flowering? Plant in the correct position, feed with a balanced fertiliser in spring, and avoid over-pruning flower buds. Are shrubs suitable for wildlife gardens? Absolutely! Many shrubs provide nectar, shelter, and berries for birds and pollinators throughout the year.
